name: Curl Silent Mode Execution Detected
description: Detects the execution of the `curl` command-line tool with the `-s` (silent) option, which suppresses output. This is often used by attackers to hide file downloads or other network activity.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Windows event ID for process creation (e.g., Sysmon Event ID 1 or Security Event ID 4688).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that executed (should be curl.exe).

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the curl.exe executable.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The complete command line used, including the silent or quiet option and any output redirection.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which curl was executed.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
